iron prices have doubled since the road south closed
a merchant caravan turned back before reaching the pass
nobody's buying grain anymore, and no one will say why
the last three shipments of salt never arrived
a peddler was selling swords out of his cart, good ones, far too cheap
the wool buyers from the east haven't shown up this season
someone bought every lantern and oil flask the general store had in one visit
the ferry operator is charging triple and claims it's not his choice
a trader offered to pay in foreign coin no one here recognized
the lumber mill cut its output in half but won't explain the reason
herb prices spiked overnight after a stranger visited the apothecary
three merchants pooled their guards for the trip north, which they've never done before
the tanner's been stockpiling leather instead of selling, says he's got a private buyer
no one's seen the usual tax collector in weeks, and a new one showed up asking odd questions
a whole wagon of tools arrived with no buyer's mark and no one claimed it
the blacksmith turned down a commission for the first time anyone can remember
the spice merchant says her suppliers have gone quiet, every single one
someone's been buying up all the rope and chain in the area
the baker raised prices and muttered something about flour shipments stopping
a caravan guard quit on the spot rather than take the western route
the fishmonger's been trading for arrows instead of coin
two competing merchants suddenly started cooperating, which has people nervous
an entire barge of goods was turned away at the river landing, no reason given
the cloth dyer packed up shop overnight and left town without a word
someone's offering premium coin for maps of the old roads, no questions asked
